notes: DEATH 12-card gravity deck. The empty ward at 3:30 AM working on Sister Beatrice: the small unfamiliarities of a building that used to be full, the cupola wind, the gurney moving in no draft.

cards.title: THE FLUORESCENT ABOVE WARD THREE
cards.flavor: Tick once on the count of seven. Tick again on the count of seven. The bulb has done this for twelve years. Tonight it is doing it into an empty room.

cards.title: THE WIND THROUGH THE CUPOLA PANE
cards.flavor: The missing pane on the east side. The wind that's been coming through it for thirty-three years tonight has a different note.

then.text: You walk past the cupola. You don't look up. The note settles back.

cards.title: THE CHART BINDER CLOSED
cards.flavor: First time in thirty years. Pearl set it on the desk at 11. The closure is the wound.

then.text: You open the binder. You read the last five names. You close it again gently.

cards.title: THE GURNEY MOVES A HALF-INCH
cards.flavor: In no draft. The wheel that always squeaked has, tonight, squeaked.

cards.title: YOU REMEMBER WARD TWO
cards.flavor: Mrs Begnaud in '99. Three weeks. She wanted Dvořák. You brought a small radio. The radio was returned to the chapel cupboard in November of that year.

cards.title: THE FAMILY ARRIVES IN THE HALL
cards.flavor: Not for a patient. For the room. The Lavignes' father was here in '07. The daughter wanted to walk the corridor once before it closes.

cards.title: DR SAVOY'S FOOTSTEPS
cards.flavor: From the south door. He came down to do this with you. He has been at the ward since '04. He is the attending who, tonight, lets you lead.

cards.title: PEARL'S CHAIR SIGHS
cards.flavor: She has stood up. She is folding the chart binder's cover closed for the second time tonight. The chair is the chair Pearl has had since 1995.

cards.title: THE CANDLE IN WARD ONE WON'T TAKE
cards.flavor: Your second match. Your third. The wick is old. The match is your hand not warm enough yet.

then.text: Fourth match. The wick catches. The flame is small.

cards.title: YOU ALMOST CLOSE YOUR EYES
cards.flavor: In the chair at the station. Pearl is in the back closet finding more candles. The corridor is the corridor. You shake yourself.

cards.title: WARD 5 SOUNDS LIKE BREATHING
cards.flavor: The room is empty. The radiator is the radiator. You name the radiator. You repeat the name. You walk past.

cards.title: DAWN IS AN HOUR AWAY
cards.flavor: By 4:30 the sky over the parking lot will start. By 5 the day shift comes in and the day shift will not, tomorrow, come in here. The vigil has to finish before the day finds the ward.

options.label: Light the last candle (cost: 1 Time; gain: −3 VIGIL, set_flag final_candle_lit)

effects.text: Last candle. Ward five. The flame catches on the first match this time.
